document.title: Literature Study
document.documentType: Literature Search Protocol and Literature Search Report
standards: Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex VIII Rule 17
Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Of The European Parliament and of The Council of 5 April 2017

sourceSummary.oneLine: Literature study for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR clinical evaluation context.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation by identifying, selecting, appraising, and summarizing scientific literature and recall information related to safety and performance of Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector and equivalent devices.
keyPoints: The device is classified as Class IIa in accordance with Medical Device Regulation (EU) 2017/745 Annex VIII Rule 17.
The device had not been marketed or sold yet in the EU at the time of the protocol.
The protocol searched PubMed, European Radiology, Medical Physics, FDA Recall, MFDS recall/suspension of sale, and MHRA alerts and recalls.
The literature search period was 2000.01 to 2022.12, with search date 2023.09 to 2023.10.
The report states that 15 documents were finally selected from PubMed, European Radiology, and Medical Physics.
Recall searches found 10 FDA recall results, 0 MFDS recall/suspension results, and 0 MHRA alerts/recalls for the stated searches.
The appraisal concluded there were no recalls related to the products and that the device has adequate performance and effectiveness for patients.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata is ambiguous: source metadata authoritySignal says MFDS/domestic, while the document text is primarily framed for CE MDR and also includes FDA, MFDS, and MHRA recall searches.
Several search-result placeholders remain in the source text, including '[문헌개수]' and '[검색연도]', indicating incomplete extracted table values.
Revision is taken from the sourcePath filename '(rev.1)' because the body text does not state a revision history value.
Document date is inferred from prepared/reviewed/approved dates of October 20, 2023; no separate document effective date is stated.

Some appraisal wording appears internally inconsistent: the protocol says exclude literatures with a score of 14 or less, but later literature with total score 14 is concluded sufficient for scientific literature databases.
